turns.user_message: I noticed I just stared at the bookshelf for ten minutes without picking anything up. It used to not bother me when I felt a little tired, but this feels different somehow. I just put everything back and walked away without really thinking about it. The whole afternoon slipped by while I sat on the couch watching the light change across the floor. I guess I just didn't have the energy to care about what I usually enjoy.

I noticed my usual evening routine feels completely hollow now. It used to not bother me when things felt a bit dull, but now it feels like a heavy fog I cannot shake off. I just go through the steps without actually seeing what I am doing with my hands. The hours blur together into one long stretch of quiet waiting for something to spark my interest. Nothing really lands the way it used to, and I am just letting the days pass.

I noticed I completely forgot to eat dinner because the idea of food just feels like a chore. It used to not bother me when I skipped a meal here and there, but this emptiness is swallowing everything whole now. I sit in the quiet and wait for a single thought to bring me any comfort, but there is just static. Every single thing I try feels completely hollow, like I am moving through thick water with no direction. I am just floating through the hours with absolutely nothing left inside to pull me forward.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something they noticed about themselves. This used to not bother the speaker.
